description.value: Newspeople, influencers, and cultural phenomena, they are ready to expose the truth by any means necessary.
Anytime the stun baton deals damage, target must make an Endurance check: DV = 10+dmg. On failure, the target is stunned for their next turn.
A concealable SMG.
Does not cause Critical Injury. Does not ablate armor. Leaves target at minimum 1hp. Counts as Basic ammo for exotic weapons
An Exotic Heavy Pistol. Target is knocked unconscious if damage dealt would reduce them to under 1 HP. Doesn't cause Critical Injuries or ablate armor. Refer to the full item description on pg. 349.
A rechargeable (1 hour) battery pack, commonly used with Exotic weapons. Contains 8 shots and can be recharged.

External Body Cyberware*. Head and Body Armor SP11 and recovers 1SP/day. Armor does not stack.

* Cyberware Armor does not function as armor in the system, please add the "Subdermal Armor" item to the character/mook to track
Armor Item for Subdermal Armor Cyberware.
Light version of an Armorjack. No armor penalty.

A bioelectrically-charged battery, capacitor, and shock pad are embedded in the hand, activating in contact with an opponent. When the user grapples an enemy, the enemy automatically takes 1d6 damage directly to their HP at the start of the user's turn. If an electrified grip is installed in 2 or more hands that are being used to grapple, this damage is changed to a flat 7 instead. This damage does not interact with armor, and if an enemy is incapacitated by it, is considered non-lethal.

Can be installed as the only piece of cyberware in a meat arm.
AI Smartphone. +2 Library Search, +2 Wardrobe & Style depending on circumstance.

information.notes:
Elizabeth is a media excelling at planning, disguise, and who began her career as a member of the Philharmonic Vampyres, rising alongside the gang as they gained noteriety through the 2030s. Like many of its other members, she graduated NCU with a degree in performance art and rapidly found the world destitute for careers in the limelight (at least those that didn't involve sex), but ripe for publicity via robbing banks with a dramatic flair.

Elizabeth is known best for the utterly impossible realities which tend to manifest around her; circumstance seeming to align "just-so" as objects and even people seem to suddenly appear in an inadvertent aid to her endeavors. She's become a pop-culture sensation, hired on jobs for actors who want spectacle and showmanship as much as results.

Personally, she is cheerful and perhaps slightly naive, preferring to complete her work without shedding blood. Even she seems sometimes surprised by the turns the world takes around her, though it's hard to say if this is actually an act, or just more of the trademark forethought and showmanship typical of the Vampyres. She enjoys her own stay in the limelight, but prefers action and reputation over direct appearance, and is actually fairly camera-shy while speaking.

Difficulty: Hard. Avoid combat and make use of lots of planning skill in addition to RP proficiency.

Style of Play: Elizabeth utilizes her technical skill, ranks in stealth, charm, and intelligence to get into all sorts of trouble, while avoiding direct combat whenever possible. She excels in situations with lots of prep time and resources, to be able to orchestrate the absurd situations she's so well known for. Note: the Panic Button is primarily for Suppressive Fire, and loaded with rubber bullets to avoid permanently hurting someone.

Character Goals: Explore the world, experience what she couldn't as a child, meet people, build her reputation as an actress, sleuth, and media, and prove to her parents that she was successful after their falling-out.

Cyberware To Aspire To: Liz is cyber-sober whenever possible; if you think about it, chroming up is a little creepy. A cyberaudio suite with a voice stress analyzer and/or audio recorder might not be amiss. Otherwise, a pair of smart glasses would do well to substitute for cybereyes with recording equipment, a HUD, and/or anti-dazzle.

In-Character Voice & Acting Inspiration: Elizabeth, Bioshock Infinite; Domino, X-Men.

Local Expert: University District.

All edgerunners included within NCG&C are wholly original, made to be played out of the box, and created using the "Complete Package" rules found in RED's Core Rulebook.

lifepath.culturalOrigin: Born in the Pacific Northwest. Moved to Night City on her parents' dime to pursue an education at NCU, graduating with a degree in performance art.
lifepath.personality: Naive and cheerful. Guileless. Very good at getting herself into trouble. Non-violent whenever possible.
lifepath.clothingStyle: Stage performance - carefully curated bright colors. Also an array of disguises.
lifepath.valueMost: Joy and passion for experiencing the wonders of the world, and helping to make them even grander than before.
lifepath.aboutPeople: Cautiously optimistic. Probably makes friends a little bit too easily.
lifepath.valuedPerson: Her aunt, who lives and works in NC as a security consultant and supports her fully in her choices, unlike the rest of her family.
lifepath.familyBackground: Corporate raised in the Pacific Northwest. Parents were supportive but inattentive, and Elizabeth never went without for anything but their attention.
lifepath.childhoodEnvironment: Raised in a sheltered environment. Parents were staunchly anti-cyber except what they had for their work, and that's largely carried over to her adult life. Suffered an unexpected pregnancy as a teenager and had an abortion, which she maintains mixed feelings about.
lifepath.familyCrisis: Parents moved her off to Night City to pursue an education in business; Liz lied and pursued one in theater instead. She's on unstable ground with her parents as a result.
lifepath.lifeGoals: Pursue her passions and experience the highs of the world. Admirably (and perhaps naively for her setting), do so without chroming up.
lifepath.friends: Her aunt, who lives in the University District in a studio. Liz often stays with her instead of at her own container.
lifepath.tragicLoveAffairs: High school sweetheart; turns out he wasn't as kind/cool/loving as she thought he was.
